# agent-friendly-guide

> A practical method — and a Claude Code skill — for making codebases
> agent-friendly: repos that LLM coding agents can use, extend, and not break.

This repo is both the method and the tooling. For an agent, the **full manual is
`GUIDELINE.md`** (there is intentionally no separate `llms-full.txt` — that would
duplicate it); the executable form is the `agent-ready` skill, shipped as a
Claude Code plugin.

## Read first

- [GUIDELINE.md](GUIDELINE.md): the full method — 8 principles, the audit checklist
  (the single source of truth the skill scores against), anti-patterns, and
  language/ecosystem notes.
- [README.md](README.md): overview, the short version, and how to install/use the skill.
- [languages/](languages/): per-ecosystem specifics (signals, doc surface,
  distribution model, traps) — `go`, `python`, `javascript`, `c`, `rust`, `jvm`,
  `shell`, `swift`, `php`, `ruby`.

## The skill

- [skills/agent-ready/SKILL.md](skills/agent-ready/SKILL.md): audits a repo,
  scaffolds its agent docs, and runs a clean-agent evaluation. Install with
  `/plugin marketplace add mixcode/agent-friendly-guide` then
  `/plugin install agent-friendly-guide@agent-friendly-guide`.

## Templates & evaluation

- [templates/](templates/): fill-in skeletons (`llms.txt`, `llms-full.txt`, `llms-full-cli.txt` for CLI/tool repos, `llms-monorepo.txt` for a monorepo root's package map, `AGENTS.txt`).
- [evaluation/clean-agent-eval.md](evaluation/clean-agent-eval.md): the clean-agent evaluation harness.

## For contributors

- [AGENTS.txt](AGENTS.txt): invariants (GUIDELINE.md is the source of truth; the
  checklist lives only there) and how to add a new skill.
